flags: BIG RBI GAME: Kyle Schwarber (6 RBI)

editorial.headline: Harper Completes First Career Cycle in Rout of Mets
editorial.subheadline: Philadelphia Phillies wins as Bryce Harper goes single, double, triple, homer.
editorial.lead_angle:
Bryce Harper dug for third, his arms churning, and slid headfirst into the history books. His eighth-inning triple completed the first cycle of his career, capping a thorough dismantling of the New York Mets by the Philadelphia Phillies, 15-3. Harper collected his single, double, and home run in his first four trips, but the three-bagger sent the Citizens Bank Park crowd into a frenzy. The rout was already on, but Harper’s hustle provided the punctuation on a punishing offensive display.

The game was put away early. New York starter Freddy Peralta never saw the fourth inning after the Phillies buried him and the Mets’ chances in a disastrous third, hanging eight runs on the board. Kyle Schwarber started the onslaught with a solo shot, his second of the night. Following a walk to Trea Turner and a single by Harper, J.T. Realmuto drove them both in with a ringing double. The crooked number chased Peralta from the mound for the loss.

While Harper provided the rare feat, Kyle Schwarber supplied the raw power. He finished the night 4-for-5 with three home runs and six runs batted in, a one-man demolition of the Mets’ pitching staff. His final blast came in the seventh, a towering shot that erased any faint comeback notions. The win, credited to Cristopher Sánchez, pushes the Phillies further ahead of their division rivals, a statement delivered by bat and brawn in front of a roaring home crowd.
editorial.reason: Hitting for the cycle happens fewer than a dozen times a season league-wide.
